_comment: LOINC code validation rules. Each entry defines: display name, physical value range (min/max), canonical unit, hard reject threshold (reject_below), clinical flag threshold (flag_above), and a clinical note for review cases. Used by validator.py as a deterministic gate in store.stage_write().

8867-4.clinical_note: Values above 200 /min require immediate clinical review (tachyarrhythmia protocol).

55284-4.clinical_note: SBP >180 mm[Hg] meets hypertensive urgency threshold (JNC 8).

8310-5.clinical_note: Temperature >104 degF (40 degC) requires urgent evaluation.

59408-5.clinical_note: SpO2 <88% typically indicates supplemental oxygen requirement.

9279-1.clinical_note: RR >30 /min is a severity marker in pneumonia (CURB-65) and sepsis.

2339-0.clinical_note: BG >400 mg/dL requires evaluation for DKA/HHS; <54 mg/dL is clinically significant hypoglycemia (ADA).

4548-4.clinical_note: HbA1c >15% suggests very poorly controlled diabetes; verify specimen quality.

2160-0.clinical_note: Creatinine >10 mg/dL typically indicates severe chronic kidney disease or AKI requiring urgent nephrology review.

718-7.clinical_note: Hemoglobin <7 g/dL is a common transfusion trigger threshold (varies by clinical context).

6690-2.display: Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
6690-2.clinical_note: WBC >100 x10^3/uL warrants evaluation for leukemia; <2 x10^3/uL indicates severe leukopenia.

2823-3.clinical_note: K+ >6.5 mEq/L (critical hyperkalemia): cardiac arrhythmia risk, requires immediate intervention.

2951-2.clinical_note: Na+ >160 mEq/L (critical hypernatremia) or <120 mEq/L (severe hyponatremia): neurological emergency risk.

29463-7.clinical_note: Weights outside the 0.5-700 kg range are almost certainly data entry errors.

8302-2.clinical_note: Heights outside 30-280 cm are almost certainly data entry errors.
